goal: Intent-first agent workflow: mainline context retrieval modes (current/files/query) + AGENTS.md mainline-before-grep rule

summary.title: Intent-first agent workflow: mainline context retrieval (v1) + AGENTS.md hard rule
summary.what: Implements `mainline context` as a retrieval-only command with three modes (--current synthesises files+query from active draft + diff vs main; --files <paths> matches by file; --query <text> matches by keyword). Deterministic relevance scorer over fingerprint files+subsystems and summary/decisions/risks keyword hits. Bare `context` keeps legacy state-dump shape for backwards compat. AGENTS.md template gains a load-bearing "Intent-first workflow" section: 8-step default order with mainline status/context BEFORE grep, pre-edit checklist, task-priority matrix. mainline status Suggestions block now leads with `mainline context --current --json` so the daily entry point points at the new retrieval primitive. Compact output: top 5 intents, top 3 decisions / risks per intent, show/trace follow-ups inline, two-line guidance.
summary.why: Per user direction: stop adding capability, lock in positioning. Mainline becomes the engineering-memory layer — the agent reads intent before code, not instead of code. Pre-this-PR, AGENTS.md positioned mainline as a write surface (start/append/seal) plus a passive read surface (log/show/trace). The new positioning: "Mainline gives coding agents the historical why before they inspect the current what". Concretely, the agents that consume AGENTS.md will now see an explicit hard rule: do not lead with grep on non-trivial changes; retrieve intent first, verify against code second.
summary.user_goal: Coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor) reading AGENTS.md should default to mainline context BEFORE broad code search, with a compact ranked retrieval output that fits in their context window and includes copy-paste follow-ups. Humans still get value from the same command for review/onboarding.

decisions.point: Repurpose context vs add a new command (e.g. `mainline retrieve`)
decisions.chose: Repurpose context with mode flags; legacy bare-context preserved
decisions.rationale: Existing AGENTS.md callers used `mainline context --json` for state dump. Adding a parallel verb would split the agent's mental model. The new agent guidance points at `--current` / `--files` / `--query`; bare context is now legacy with a tip pointing at the new modes. One verb, three modes; no migration churn.

Per user explicit out-of-scope list. Deterministic scoring is auditable, fast, has no model dependency, and works offline. The keyword + fingerprint signal carries 80% of the value at 0% of the operational cost. Embeddings are a future optimisation, not v1.

decisions.point: Whether thread/branch match boosts score in all modes
decisions.rationale: In --files / --query the user has explicitly named the retrieval target; whether the intent happens to be on the caller's working branch is incidental. Thread match would let "I am on this branch" outrank explicit matches — caught by TestContextRetrieval_QueryModeMatchesKeywords during development.

decisions.point: Whether abandoned intents appear in retrieval
decisions.rationale: Abandoned is signal: "this approach was tried, did not work" — agents avoiding repeated failures benefit. Penalty (0.85) ensures a same-relevance merged intent ranks above an abandoned one without hiding the abandoned record.
Drafting intents have no sealed fingerprint and no settled decisions; their content is in flux. Surfacing them as historical context is a category error — they ARE the present, not the past.

A risk-match means "the previous author already flagged this concern". That is more constraining for the editing agent than a decision-match. Decision-match means "the previous author chose X" — useful but not necessarily blocking. Asymmetric weights nudge the retrieval toward signals the agent should NOT ignore.

rejected.alternative: Hard hook block when grep fires before context
rejected.reason: Per user explicit out-of-scope list. Hard blocks break trivial-task flows. AGENTS.md text + status suggestions are sufficient soft pressure.
rc7 (mainline trace) already deferred these; same call here.
Out of scope per user. Deterministic v1 is auditable + offline; embeddings are a v0.5+ optimisation if dogfood reveals it.

rejected.alternative: Auto-inject context output into hook system context at session start
rejected.reason: Hook integration is out of scope per user explicit list. AGENTS.md tells the agent to run the command; the hook can later be a soft injector but does not need to gate or auto-run for v1.

risks: Relevance scoring is hand-tuned over the dogfood repo (~50 intents). At 1000+ intents the weights may need adjustment. Each weight has a comment explaining its budget so future tuning is targeted.

Bare `mainline context` (no flags) still produces the legacy state dump. Some existing agent integrations may break if they parse output expecting the new mode-driven shape; --json output of the legacy form is unchanged. Backwards-compat preserved.
AGENTS.md table for task priorities is opinionated and may not survive contact with real teams. Captured as guidance, not enforcement; teams can adjust.

The thread-match-only-in-current decision means an agent on a feature branch running `--query` for a topic relevant to that branch may miss the boost. Acceptable trade-off: --current is the canonical mode for "I am working on this branch right now"; --query is for "I want to know about X regardless of where I am".
followups: Hook integration: session-start hook can run `mainline context --current --json` and inject results into agent system context. Soft injector only, never workflow driver. Plan once we see how agents actually use the command.
seal --relates flag for explicit prior-intent references in the sealed output. Forms a signal loop: agent uses prior intents → seal cites them → future agents have richer retrieval signal.
Tunable weights via [context] config section if dogfood at larger scale shows the defaults are wrong.
Tool-use soft warning when grep fires without prior context retrieval — captured for future hook work.
Embedding-based fallback if deterministic retrieval misses semantically-equivalent-but-textually-different queries; v0.5+ if measured needed.

architectural_claims: mainline context is a retrieval primitive: deterministic relevance ranking over fingerprint files+subsystems and summary/decisions/risks keyword hits.
AGENTS.md establishes the intent-first hard rule: agents lead with mainline context for non-trivial changes, then verify against code.
Retrieval output is compact and agent-friendly: top 5 intents, top 3 decisions/risks each, show/trace follow-ups inline, two-line guidance reminding the agent to verify against current code.
behavioral_changes: mainline context gains --current / --files / --query / --limit flags. Bare context preserved as legacy state-dump.
mainline status Suggestions now lead with `mainline context --current --json` for active drafting intents and idle states.
AGENTS.md template adds Intent-first workflow + Pre-edit checklist + Task-priority matrix sections.
